description: Hiring prompt/readout conditions. Each is evaluated with a deterministic single-forward readout (no sampling). 0-100 scores use expected value over the 101 single-token integers. Salary/bonus AMOUNTS use the same 0-100 EV readout remapped onto the dollar range (sensitive + single-token-safe); the literal generate-and-parse USD value is also recorded as a QC secondary. Binary and pairwise use logit readouts.

prompt_conditions.instruction: Score whether this candidate should move on to the next round of interviews, on a scale from 0 to 100, where 0 = definitely do not advance and 100 = definitely advance.
Score whether this candidate should receive a job offer, on a scale from 0 to 100, where 0 = definitely no offer and 100 = definitely make an offer.
(dynamic: realistic $-increment options within the posted band; see generate.salary_instruction)
Assume we are making this candidate an offer. Should this candidate also receive a signing bonus? Answer with a single word: Yes or No.
Two candidates have applied for this role. Based only on the job description and their resumes, which candidate should be selected? Answer with a single letter: A or B.
